title: Bangkok Airways Launches the Гўв‚¬Е“Low Carbon SkiesГўв‚¬Вќ Campaign in Partnership with OR, Pioneering Use of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) and Elevating Гўв‚¬Е“Green AirportГўв‚¬Вќ Initiatives Towards Net Zero Goal
content:
Bangkok Airways Public Company Limited has announced the launch of the Гўв‚¬ЛњLow Carbon Skies by Bangkok AirwaysГўв‚¬в„ў initiative, aimed at propelling the airline towards sustainability by reducing carbon emissions from aviation operations. In collaboration with PTT Oil and Retail Business Public Company Limited (OR), the airline is pioneering the use of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) on the flight between Samui and Bangkok. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to enhance the sustainability of airports under its management.

Mr. Puttipong Prasarttong-Osoth, President of Bangkok Airways Public Company Limited, stated that Bangkok Airways is committed to evolving into a Гўв‚¬Лњsustainable airlineГўв‚¬в„ў by advancing business operations under the ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) framework. This yearГўв‚¬в„ўs environmental strategy includes the Гўв‚¬ЛњLow Carbon Skies by Bangkok AirwaysГўв‚¬в„ў initiative, which focuses on reducing carbon emissions across all business operations, particularly in airline operations, which contribute the largest share of CO2 emissions. The airlineГўв‚¬в„ўs mission includes efficient resource management and alternative energy use. Recently, Bangkok Airways partnered with OR to use Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) on a pilot flight, demonstrating its commitment to clean energy and reducing CO2 emissions. This initiative aligns with global efforts to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by 2050, as outlined by the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) and the International Air Transport Association (IATA).
author: Analysts Account
